summary: Work on the interceptor was completed Wednesday. Two more interceptors are expected by mid-October, according to the agency. title: Letters to the Editor
summary: People need to remember that several dozen brown bears live in the foothills and mountains of Anchorage and ought to carry pepper spray, make noise and be aware, said assistant biologist Jessy Coltrane, with the Alaska Department of Fish and Game. The big omnivores will feed on dead animals they find -- whether killed by hunters or natural causes -- and react defensively if people or their dogs come too close, she said. "But the worst thing that could possibly happen is that this instills a paranoia out there," Coltrane added. "Every day we have hundreds and hundreds and probably thousands of people hike around Alaska, and most of those people never see a bear, and the reason is the bears are moving away from them."
